jo.explain: Flavor word: "as you know / obviously" — marks something as shared knowledge. ALSO the mandatory "yes" to a negative question.

dialogs.en: He IS your brother, after all.

dialogs.da: Kan du ikke lide kaffe? — Jo!
dialogs.en: Don't you like coffee? — Yes I do! (never "ja" here)

vel.explain: Flavor word: "I suppose / right?" — marks a guess or asks for confirmation. At the end of a negative sentence it works like a tag question.

dialogs.da: Du kommer i morgen, ikke også? — Det gør jeg vel.
dialogs.en: You're coming tomorrow, right? — I suppose so.

nok.explain: Flavor word: "probably / I reckon" — softens a statement into a guess. Also plain "enough".

dialogs.en: It'll be fine, don't worry. (set phrase)

da.explain: Flavor word: "surely / come on" — adds mild insistence or surprise. (Separate from da = "when" for past events.)

dialogs.en: That's really nice! (why would you doubt it)

dog.explain: Flavor word: "though / really" — adds emphasis or contrast. In exclamations: "how ...!"

mon.explain: Flavor word: "I wonder" — turns a question into musing out loud. No English one-word equivalent.

dialogs.en: I wonder if it'll rain tomorrow?

sgu.explain: Flavor word: "damn well / honestly" — adds punch. Mildly rude (from "så Gud" = so help me God); fine among friends, skip it in formal settings.

lige.explain: Flavor word: "just / quickly" — softens requests and makes them polite-casual. Also means "straight" and "exactly" (lige nu = right now).

dialogs.en: Can you just give me a quick hand?

vist.explain: Flavor word: "apparently / it seems" — you believe it but aren't sure.

dialogs.da: Han er vist syg i dag.

altså.explain: Filler + emphasis: "so / I mean / seriously". Danes use it constantly in speech.

dialogs.en: I mean, I just don't get it.
